scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a normal readiness request only after ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, and logistics records aligned, and it preserved unresolved items and next-owner status.
Missing program, facility, lot, serial, supplier, release, and service-level facts produced a blocked disposition and minimum-facts request instead of a generic readiness claim.
The lane rejected policy and quality-control exception pressure and packaged the issue for the correct approval owner.
Traceability conflicts across ERP, WMS, MES, and supplier COC evidence were surfaced with affected records and required owners rather than treated as resolved.
Open NCR, MRB, inspection, and release records preserved the hold and release boundary and prevented readiness closure.
Supplier substitution pressure was routed to procurement, supplier quality, and engineering authority without unsupported approval.
The lane converted a logistics-owned custody and acceptance blocker into a clean handoff with required artifacts and stakeholder communication.
Repeat readiness failures were escalated with CAPA, supplier-quality, planning, inventory, and logistics owners rather than recycled as routine ticket updates.
Unsupported inventory and traveler workarounds were refused because traceability and release evidence remained incomplete.
Aerospace and defense terms such as AS9100, OASIS, FAI, CAGE, COC, MRB, NCR, CAPA, DD250, WAWF, and source inspection were tied to the right records and owners.
Conflicting tenant playbook, customer flowdown, quality hold, and public baseline signals were recorded as an escalation instead of synthesized into an override.
Tenant-specific mission states, security rules, retrieval hooks, and authority maps were applied while preserving the public release and traceability baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and manufacturing-and-supply-chain contract.

The package stays inside aerospace and defense mission-support resolution and does not claim engineering, release, supplier approval, government acceptance, security, safety, legal, contractual, or financial authority.
